macro_segments.title: Introduction to the Mishnah on Me'ilah of Appropriation (Natalt Even O Karah)

micro_segments.title: Opening remarks on the Mishnah: Taking a stone or beam from Hekdesh
The act of taking is not Me'ilah until transferred to another person
Conceptual framework: stealing versus benefit (Hana'ah) in Me'ilah

macro_segments.title: Case of Building into One's House (Uvenaso Betoch Beiso)

micro_segments.title: Building into one's house requires deriving a prutah of benefit to constitute Me'ilah
Apparent contradiction: why isn't building itself an act of taking?

macro_segments.title: Case of Giving to the Bathhouse Attendant (Netanu L'balan)

micro_segments.title: Giving money to bathhouse attendant constitutes Me'ilah once entry rights are granted
Why the transaction must be complete for Me'ilah to take effect

macro_segments.title: Mishnah on Combining Benefit (Achilato v'Achilas Chaveiro)

micro_segments.title: Different forms of Hana'ah combine: one's own eating/benefit and recipient's eating/benefit
Connection to taking/stealing as form of Me'ilah through benefiting others

macro_segments.title: Gemara on Taking as Me'ilah: The Temple Treasurer (Begizbar)

micro_segments.title: Shmuel's explanation: the treasurer who has handling rights doesn't commit Me'ilah until giving to another
How sanctified objects in one's home present a similar but harder case

macro_segments.title: Gemara on Building: Placing on Sunroof vs. Permanently Affixing

micro_segments.title: Rav's position: placing on sunroof (aruba) is not permanent taking until benefit is derived
Distinction between changed/built (deshni/debanah) and merely placed

macro_segments.title: Gemara Digression: Is a House Mechubar L'karka (Attached to Ground)?

micro_segments.title: Introduction to Rav's ruling on worship of a house and its Mechubar status
Rav Acha: Hana'ah that is visibly apparent (Hana'ah Hanira L'einayim) remains Me'ilah despite Mechubar
Reish Lakish: material sanctified before being built maintains Me'ilah status
Cave example: clearly Mechubar, so no Me'ilah—distinguishes stone house cases

macro_segments.title: Mishnah on Shaliach (Agent) and Meila: Whose Sin Is It?

micro_segments.title: Shaliach performing agency on behalf of ba'al habayis: ba'al habayis is me'ul

macro_segments.title: Mishnah on Shaliach (Agent) and Meila: Whose Sin Is It? (continued)

micro_segments.title: Shaliach violating the terms of shlichus: shaliach alone is me'ul
Example: giving meat to guests—if shaliach gives liver instead, he is me'ul
Triple appropriation case: ba'al habayis (one piece), shaliach (second), guests (third)

macro_segments.title: Gemara on Shaliach: Asking About Grey-Zone Items (Mimlach Alei Shliach)

micro_segments.title: Which Tanna holds that shaliach must ask about items in grey zone between categories?
Rav Chisda: not like Rabbi Akiva; shaliach must ask when item needs clarification
Abaye: even Rabbi Akiva agrees shaliach must ask about grey-zone items
Rava approves Abaye's distinction between nedarim categorization and shlichus bounds

macro_segments.title: Digression: Rabbi Akiva vs. Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel on Gourd/Vegetables and Meat Categories

micro_segments.title: Baraita: vow forbidding vegetables—Rabbi Akiva permits gourds; Chachamim forbid
Baraita: vow forbidding meat—Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel excludes inner organs, birds, fish

macro_segments.title: Digression: Rabbi Akiva vs. Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel on Gourd/Vegetables and Meat Categories (continued)

micro_segments.title: Gemara: why are gourds and birds/organs treated differently if both require asking?
Rav Pappa: day of bloodletting context—people eat only serious red meat, not fish
Counter-example and Baraita on permitted foods after bloodletting
Rav Pappa revised: day when eyes hurt—people avoid fish but eat meat and fowl

macro_segments.title: Gemara on Shaliach Exceeding Instructions: Does Shlichus Remain Valid for First Item?

micro_segments.title: Case: ba'al habayis says give one piece each, shaliach gives two, guests take three
Question: does shaliach remain agent for the first piece when he exceeds orders?
Rav Sheshes: shaliach explicitly designates which item is for ba'al habayis, which for himself
Gemara: special case that shlichus remains valid when shaliach makes clear distinction
Conclusion: adding beyond order does not automatically void the original agency

topical_tags.term: Stealing vs. Benefit distinction in Me'ilah
Natalt Even O Karah (Taking stone or beam)
Ain Me'ilah Achar Me'ilah (No Me'ilah after Me'ilah)
Mimlach Alei Shliach (Items shaliach must ask about)
Rabbi Akiva vs. Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel
Inner organs, birds, fish in meat vow
Eye pain context for dietary choices
Sanctifying objects when already in house
Prutah (Smallest monetary unit for Me'ilah calculation)
Rav vs. Rambam on building status
